purpose: Translate the published alginate-laminin-PEDOT:PSS hydrogel method into LIMINA H-A sentinel defaults without treating literature targets as measured local data.

literature_recipe_anchor.alginate_source: PRONOVA UP VLVG sodium alginate, Mw < 75 kDa in source paper
literature_recipe_anchor.crosslinker: 183 mM calcium sulfate dihydrate dispersed in 1x DMEM
literature_recipe_anchor.gelation: syringe-mixed precursor/crosslinker, cast into controlled-thickness mold, 1 h gelation
literature_recipe_anchor.conductive_phase: commercial PEDOT:PSS Clevios PH 100 water dispersion
literature_recipe_anchor.laminin: Engelbreth-Holm-Swarm laminin, source paper used Sigma-Aldrich L2020
literature_recipe_anchor.cell_culture_loading: 0.6 wt percent PEDOT:PSS used for all cell-culture data in the source paper
literature_recipe_anchor.material_characterization_loadings: 0.9 and 1.2 wt percent PEDOT:PSS characterized for optical, mechanical, and electrochemical trends
literature_recipe_anchor.thickness_window_um: 150 to 400 um for optical/culture-relevant thin hydrogel tests; 1 mm hydrogel pieces used beside cultures; 10 mm mold used for bulk electrochemistry in source setup
literature_recipe_anchor.pre_measurement_conditioning: overnight incubation in DMEM/F12 or relevant cell culture medium before electrochemical measurement
literature_recipe_anchor.published_performance_context: 1-10 kPa mechanical range, >28 day hiPSC-derived cortical neuron cytocompatibility, stable electrochemistry over 80 cycles

h_a_interpretation.lead_preference: 0.6 wt percent PEDOT:PSS is the first LIMINA lead because it is the source paper's cell-culture loading and should preserve optical/biological margin.
h_a_interpretation.challenge_role: 1.2 wt percent is a stress comparator for conductivity-versus-transparency and toxicity, not a first-claim lead.
h_a_interpretation.stop_condition: Any H-A medium drift, visible shedding, swelling, delamination, or transparency failure stops H-B/H-C advancement.
